identity.title: Define and prove resource and durability models

authorization.reference: Operator authorized Phase 0 planning, requested WP-FF-008 through WP-FF-011 as detailed no-context STUB packets linked to the canonical design and readiness topics, and required the stubs to be committed and pushed before WP-FF-003 implementation begins.

blockers: WP-FF-003 executable gate bootstrap must be VALIDATED before compiled models or tests are added.
WP-FF-005 must establish versioned identity, cancellation, error, process, and public-contract foundations consumed by these models.
Supported filesystem capability profiles and exact Phase 0 proof platforms must be frozen at activation.
lifecycle.next_action: After prerequisites pass, refresh current structured-concurrency, resource-admission, crash-consistency, and filesystem research; freeze the state models and proof profiles; promote the STUB; then implement deterministic model/property/fault tests before integrating the models into broad feature code.

scope.summary: Turn the design's atomic resource-vector, byte-credit, cancellation, durability-position, staged-commit, and filesystem-capability rules into versioned contracts and executable models that broad feature work must consume.
in_scope: Define the immutable ResourceVector dimensions, per-job/global limits, all-or-nothing central grant, deterministic fairness/tie-break policy, RAII ownership, cancellation, and release semantics.

Define byte-credit acquisition, ownership transfer, simultaneous input/output reservation, spill behavior, item/byte queue occupancy, loss policy, and upstream backpressure for every variable-size stage.
Define separate received, validated-written-contiguous, and durable-contiguous positions and prohibit resume state beyond the durable prefix.

Define finite state models for job/cancel, admission, fragment/durability, sink, FFmpeg resource use, commit/archive, filesystem capability, and recovery transitions touched by the Phase 2 proof slice.
Define same-filesystem staged commit and startup reconciliation for every PREPARED, RENAMED, ARCHIVED, and CLEANED crash prefix, including torn/duplicate/reordered journal records.
Define supported filesystem capability profiles for atomic replace, synchronization, leases/locking, path confinement, cross-volume behavior, and explicit degraded or rejected modes.

Implement deterministic model, property, saturation, cancellation, and fault tests that falsify leaks, over-admission, starvation, hidden unbounded queues, false durability, corrupt resume, and false success.
Expose stable structured model traces, invariant IDs, occupancy, grants, transition reasons, and residual uncertainty for no-context diagnosis.
non_goals: Implementing the full scheduler, downloader, archive backend, FFmpeg adapter, or every product state machine.
Introducing adaptive concurrency before fixed deterministic policy passes correctness, saturation, cancellation, and equivalent-work gates.
Claiming universal filesystem atomicity, universal termination, zero data loss, or correctness beyond declared assumptions and capability profiles.
Allowing independent semaphores, optimistic checkpoints, unbounded queues, or watcher acknowledgement to become the product contract.
Reducing protocol, live, sink, collection, archive, or recovery scope to simplify the model.
dependencies: Canonical architecture, PR, and triggered deep gates implemented by fforager-xtask
Controlled clock, deterministic scheduler, model/property-test, and fault-injection support in fforager-testkit
Current platform documentation for Windows and Unix filesystem/process guarantees

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Versioned resource and byte-credit contracts define every admitted dimension, item/byte bound, ownership transfer, coupled reservation, fairness rule, cancellation outcome, loss policy, and observable occupancy used by the Phase 2 proof slice.

Deterministic model/property tests prove all-or-nothing resource grants, no hold-and-wait across scheduler permits, no permit/credit leak on success/error/panic/cancellation, bounded queues and memory, cancellation progress, and the declared starvation/fairness bound.

The durability model separates received, validated-written-contiguous, and durable-contiguous positions; every generated trace resumes at or before durable_contiguous and no success trace precedes output validation plus archive reconciliation.

Fault injection covers every PREPARED, RENAMED, ARCHIVED, and CLEANED prefix plus torn, duplicate, reordered, checksum-invalid, cross-volume, stale-lease, collision, partial-cleanup, and interrupted-migration cases with an idempotent declared recovery or fail-closed result.

Supported filesystem profiles state exact atomic-replace, file/directory sync, lease/lock, rooted no-follow path, cross-volume, and degraded-mode guarantees for each tested platform; unsupported security-sensitive confinement fails closed.

Structured traces and machine-readable reports let a no-context model identify the invariant, state, resource vector, credit owner, queue occupancy, durable prefix, injected fault, recovery action, and residual uncertainty for every failed case.
FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, triggered model/fault/saturation deep gates, and an independent adversarial review pass on the same source and fixture manifest.

checks_run: git status --porcelain=v2 --branch: main at 447b4dbb0d64df56b3c956d8f311ff24f5b51c50 before packet creation
git worktree list --porcelain: one main worktree; no packet worktree created
blockers: Packet is a STUB and does not authorize implementation.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Wait for prerequisites, freeze proof profiles, and promote before implementing contracts or tests.

not_proven: Atomic resource admission or bounded byte flow
Crash-safe durability, resume, commit, archive, or filesystem behavior

promotion_requirements: WP-FF-003 and WP-FF-005 prerequisite evidence is accepted.
Current source-level research is recorded for structured concurrency, coupled-resource admission, byte backpressure, crash-consistent journals, and Windows/Unix filesystem guarantees.
The initial fixed fairness policy, resource dimensions, byte stages, filesystem profiles, and finite state models are frozen.
Every model has assumptions, invariant IDs, generated transitions, timeout, failure oracle, and report schema.
No broad feature implementation depends on an unproven model.

documentation_topics.heading: End-to-end byte credits and durability positions
documentation_topics.use: credit ownership and three durability positions

documentation_topics.heading: Scheduler and Resource Governance
documentation_topics.use: atomic vector, fixed policy, task ownership, fairness, cancellation, backpressure, saturation

documentation_topics.heading: Persistence, Resume, and Crash Recovery
documentation_topics.use: journal, durable state, staged commit, reconciliation, filesystem capability

readiness_matrix_items.required_resolution: Atomic resource-vector broker, byte-credit queues, structured ownership, cancellation, and fixed policies before adaptive behavior.
Durable staged commit, filesystem capability, and startup reconciliation for every state prefix.
Byte credits plus received, validated/written, and durable-contiguous checkpoints.
Exact local-filesystem guarantees with explicit reject/degrade behavior.
FFmpeg process slots plus coupled CPU, memory, disk, and pipe budgets.
Versioned job/cancel, admission, fragment/durability, sink, FFmpeg, and commit/archive models.

readiness_matrix_items.gate: Atomic resource-vector and byte-credit invariants with executable saturation and cancellation tests
Commit/archive/resume fault injection across every durable state prefix

base_scope: Versioned durability and recovery state models

high_roi_additions.addition: Build one reusable controlled-clock schedule explorer for resource, retry, lease, timeout, and cancellation races.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Those races already share time and interleaving requirements, so one harness reduces later packet cost.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Real-time tests would be flaky and miss boundary schedules.
high_roi_additions.validation: Seed replay is deterministic and mutation cases that leak a grant or skip cancellation are detected.

high_roi_additions.addition: Emit a compact state/credit timeline and counterexample minimization in failed model reports.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: It makes failures reproducible by operators and no-context models while the transition system is already instrumented.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Large property-test traces are otherwise hard to diagnose.
high_roi_additions.validation: A known failing fixture produces the same minimized trace across reruns.

high_roi_additions.addition: Use the exact same durable-prefix enum and recovery oracle in WP-FF-008 and WP-FF-011.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: It prevents three implementations of crash semantics and makes vertical-slice integration cheaper.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Archive, commit, and end-to-end tests could otherwise disagree.
high_roi_additions.validation: Cross-package golden fixtures deserialize and return identical next actions.

reused_systems: WP-FF-003 executable gate and report infrastructure
fforager-core state-machine and resource ownership boundary
fforager-testkit deterministic clocks and fault injection
gaps_closed: Independent semaphores do not define coupled admission
Byte ownership and queue bounds are not executable
Resume checkpoints may otherwise outrun durable data
Commit/archive crash prefixes lack executable recovery oracles
Filesystem guarantees and degraded modes are not frozen

red_team.risk: Atomic admission deadlocks or starves large composite requests.
failure_scenarios: task holds memory while waiting for FFmpeg slot
small jobs indefinitely bypass large vector
red_team.verification: Schedule exploration and saturation tests assert no partial grant, leak, or starvation outside declared bound.

red_team.risk: A transform temporarily owns input and output without enough credits.
red_team.verification: Dishonest lengths and expansion-ratio fixtures remain within declared memory/disk bounds.

red_team.risk: Journal state advances beyond durable bytes.
red_team.verification: Crash injection after every write/sync/record step never resumes beyond durable_contiguous.

red_team.risk: Filesystem behavior is assumed portable when it is not.
red_team.verification: Windows and Unix profiles run exact positive and negative path/durability fixtures.

red_team.risk: Model proof overclaims real implementation behavior.
red_team.verification: Architecture/deep gate fails on unmapped transition or unregistered queue.

required_proof_classes: Versioned schemas and golden transition fixtures
Windows and Unix filesystem capability probes
verification_needs.closure_rule: No PASS while any state transition lacks declared preconditions/invariants/postconditions, any variable-size queue lacks item and byte bounds, any generated trace permits false durability/success, or platform proof is missing for a claimed profile.
